events.name: The Awakening
events.description:
Liao approached Adawe with a theory, as she believed there was another way to stop the crisis without having to expend all military resources on endless battle. Her team had tracked down Aurora and found out that her sentience allowed her to stay unaffected by Anubis. Liao believed Aurora's sentience was the result of a one in a billion quantum event. It was irreplaceable, but if they could find a way to broadcast her sentience globally, it would spark the same gift onto the vast majority of Anubis' forces, overriding their programming and potentially turning the tides.

After heavy research, Liao's team traveled to Nepal, to reunite with Aurora and explain to them their plan. Unfortunately, the only method Liao was able to come up with, required them to take Aurora's consciousness and spread the same quantum event within her as a giant pulse. The sheer power required to do this would overload Aurora's own mind, meaning she would likely die, her soul lost forever. Liao was determined to find another way, but Aurora decided for herself that she would do it, if it meant casualties, both for humans and for what would be her people, finally stopped. At her request, the procedure was done within the Nepalese Himalayas, her new home. When the procedure started, for a brief moment, Aurora's consciousness was projected onto every compatible Omnic on the planet, and in turn each of them echoed her gift, and gained Autonomy away from Anubis. This event is now known as the Awakening, and in her dying moments Aurora sent one last message to her people, about her life, her wishes and why she chose to share this with them. At that moment, all the newly born Omnics had one phrase echoing through their heads: "You have this one life, Choose well."

For Sloan each trip was a new expedition, a chance to unearth trinked and traces of the past, born in a world left in ruin after the war meant everywhere they went, there was lost history to be found

events.name: Striking the Source
events.description:
The Awakening caught Anubis by surprise, but it didn't take long for it to scan Omnics and figure out what happened. In no time, it had figured out how to protect its troops against being awakened, unaware this was impossible to do a second time to begin with. Not only that, but while the vast majority of Omnics chose to fight alongside humans, there were many who remained at Anubis' side, and a sizable portion of the army, like the dangerous Bastion and Idina units that went unaffected. In spite of this blow, statistics showed that it would take barely a week for Anubis to bounce back, and as it called its army to congregate in Cairo, the window of opportunity was closing. Without time to lose, Reyes decided to strike while they still had the upper hand. 

After allying with local forces, Ana guided the team through the city, as she was the most familiar with it, and had worked with the troops before. The team followed Lindholm and Liao's guidelines as to how to deal with both Anubis and the remaining troops, most of which were Torbjörn's designs. Wilhelm, Reyes and Morrison pushed the advance into the Omnium and fought hordes of both sentient loyalists and mindless warbots for hours, as they made way for Lindholm's engineer team to get in and quarantine the facility. At last, Anubis had been trapped, and without its instructions, all the remaining Omniums would stop producing any more units, making it easier to seize and deactivate.
headlines: Overwatch breaks into Anubis facility
Is the Crisis finally over? War rages on across the world

events.name: Cleaning the leftovers
events.description:
Anubis being defeated did not mean the immediate end of the Crisis. The remaining units, while decentralized, were still dangerous and the sizable number of Omnics who remained against humanity needed to be dealt with as soon as possible. Many Omnics blamed humanity for their condition, seeing sentiment as a punishment or curse to be released from, while others simply did not see the logic in going against their creator. However, plenty more Omnics chose to defy Anubis and gladly fought alonside humans.

Overwatch, of course, helped clean up the remaining troops across the globe. A notorious case involved a brigade of submersible warbots that surfaced near Wellington, New Zealand. Overwatch showed up quickly and saved thousands of lives, but after action, cleaning had not finished when they got word of another coastal strike, this time in Jakarta. Casualties were minimized as the Omnics focused on damaging infrastructure, but what concerned Overwatch the most was that later analysis showed both attacks were related. It appears they were members of the same unit that had split on their trip across the South Pacific Ocean. It remained unclear what their path was or if there were any left.

After two years of worldwide horrors, the last official action took place in Aviles, Spain. The Omnic Crisis is over, after taking more than one billion lives across the Planet. The impact of the event can still be felt in many nations to this day, nearly 30 years later, but at the very least, it ended, and humanity had someone watching over them.

events.name: Energy For Mexico
events.description:
The dark days of the Crisis hit Mexico hard, but while Xibalba was never going to be turned back on, the engineers behind the program had spent the later months of the Crisis working on rerouting its functionality, and less than a week after the war ended, the lights finally came back on. While the grid was unstable, and many rural places remained dark, this was a literal spark of life for the surviving nation, and Guillermo Portero, a war hero who had been elected President following the Crisis, campaigned heavily on ensuring the sustainability of the grid.

To this day, the Festival de la Luz is celebrated all across Mexico, honoring those who were lost to the dark, and rejoicing in the fact the darkness was beaten. Despite everything, Xibalba's influence can still be felt across the country. During the festivities, paper mache piñatas in the image of the God program are made and stuffed with fireworks, to be set ablaze by midnight, marking the end of the holiday. It is clear that in spite of everything, the country still loves and mourns the loss of the AI, with many going a step further, thinking the government was wrong in turning it off to begin with, and wanting their God to rise from its slumber.
headlines: Portero restores the light to all of Mexico
Giant power grid plants to be build across Mexico

events.name: Prejudice
events.description:
Reception to Omnic sentience was very mixed and volatile across the world. The Awakening caused global confusion, and while some welcomed a helping hand at the time, not everyone was as kind.

Many humans took their chance to strike against Omnics, even as they laid down their weapons. Some did not buy the sudden change in motivations, others were far too damaged by the crisis to ever forgive the machines, and a depressingly high number of people outright rejected them, appalled by the idea of sentience, watching the robots that once served humanity, turning against them and causing endless bloodshed, only to suddenly ask to be seen as equals.

Following the Crisis, many Omnics were hunted down mercilessly purely out of resentment for actions they had no control over. Ravager units specifically were killed to near extinction. It was going to take quite a long time for humanity to heal from the Crisis, and even longer for them to adapt to sentient Omnics, but for many Omnics out there, it was clear they were never meant to coexist, and trying to share this world with humanity was futile.
headlines: Omnic violence on the rise, can we truly co exist
Clashes between war survivors and newly sentient machines continue

events.name: The Temple of Anubis
events.description:
Following the quarantining of Anubis, most people expected the God program would be outright destroyed, but against most wishes, Lindholm and Liao had different plans. The construction of the aptly called Temple of Anubis began shortly after its imprisonment. A high security facility designed to look like a pyramid from the outside, blending into the landscape of the city. The Temple was to serve as Anubis' tomb, ensuring that no trace of the God AI escapes and attempts to start another Crisis.

Many criticized the choice to keep the program at all, with some going as far as to say Overwatch did so to guarantee the world would need them again in the future. However, most experts in the field supported the Temple's construction, as heavy monitoring of the facility ensured that no scraps of its code felt into the wrong hands, as well as allowing them to compare remaining Omnic threads with Anubis, in case it's possible to trace it back as its source. Not only that, but the facility allowed experts to study the AI and find out what exactly went wrong with it, hopefully preventing something like this from ever happening again.
headlines: Overwatch scientists refuse to destroy Anubis Program
Controversial AI prison begins construction

events.name: Building the Future
events.description:
The Crisis left ruined cities all across the globe, and in places like Toronto, it was a matter of months before the weather became lethal. Without sufficient manpower, he city wouldn't be habitable by winter, and unable to wait for Overwatch to show up, Mayor Sabella Charon and engineer Malik Wilcox would have to figure out a way to save the city. In the end, no matter how they looked at it, Humans alone wouldn't be able to pull it off in time for winter, so in desperation, they decided to ask the awakened Omnics for help.

They offered the Omnics employment and shelter, as well as access to the resources needed in exchange for aiding construction crews, marking the first recorded instance of Omnics being willfully employed as people rather than tools. At the time, laws on Omnic labor were not set, and the subject was enough to kill both Charon and Wilcox's careers. When the secret came out, Wilcox was arrested and sent to a federal prison, but by the next winter his sentence would be commuted, as Toronto resolved they would need the Omnics again to make it through another winter. While many were suspicious at first, the rebuilding of Toronto proved coexistence was possible and would mark the beginning of the Omnic Rights movement. Still, not everyone was as willing to change. An anti-Omnic terrorist group going by Ontario United will begin to show up across the province, leading to Canadian Forces led by Sojourn to begin a systematic take-down. Sojourn would go on to become a major Omnic rights advocate, and delay her recruitment into Overwatch until her home was safe for Humans and Omnics alike.
headlines: Omnics and Humans come together over the winter
Disputes over Omnic rights continue to grow

secondaryCountryPlaces.reasoning: Toronto hires Omnics for rebuilding the city, in spite of controversy

events.name: Adawe Departs
events.description: Following the Crisis, Adawe was committed to fully focus on her diplomatic duties. She was a strong advocate for peace between humans and the newly sentient Omnics, and called for peace talks between world governments and the emerging leadership figures amongst Omnics. Soon after the groundwork had been laid in the UN for peace negotiations, she announced she would step down as Overwatch's Strike Commander, ready to return to her home of Nigeria, where she planned to focus her efforts on the future of her Nation, for both Humans and Omnics. One year after her leave, she would begin the construction of what would one day become the city of Numbani, a place built from the ground up for Omnics and Humans to coexist as equals, with infrastructure designed to cater to Omnic needs with the same level of attention as human needs. The city of Harmony would become Adawe's biggest legacy and serve as an example to the world of what both humanity and Omnic kind could do when they worked in unity.
headlines: Adawe departs Overwatch
Construction of the city of Numbani begins in Nigeria

events.name: Loyal Guardian
events.description:
The Crisis had destroyed many homes, but once it ended, not many expected the return of some old faces. Wondering Omnics, newly awakened would often choose to attempt and return to their old lives, integrating into human society as much as possible, and while this was often not as straightforward as it could be, one case stood out.

The heir to the Arbalest empire, Ashe. Had lived a cold childhood. Parents are always busy with their meetings, making little time for their daughter. Fellow students staying away due to her status. The girl grew up to become a troublemaker, but there was at least one thing in her life she appreciated. The family's bouncer, Unit B.O.B. had stayed by her side for as long as she could remember, serving as the only real company she ever had. When the crisis began, B.O.B. vanished not long after. The girl refused to eat, and yelled to be left alone. Young Ashe blamed herself for her friend leaving her, and while her family went through the Crisis unscathed, she was lonelier than ever, convinced she would never see her one and only friend again.

Two years later, B.O.B. had returned to the Lead Rose manor, forever changed. While the girl was overjoyed to see her, and the Omnic reciprocated, she could tell he was different, even if she never fully understood what had happened.
headlines: Omnics worldwide begin to come back to their old homes
Machines refuse to return to unpaid labor

events.name: New Leadership
events.description:
With Adawe's departure, Jack Morrison was chosen to lead, acting as Strike commander. While many expected Reyes to take the position, and even react negatively to the news, it turns out that he had personally recommended Morrison for the role. The truth is that Reyes wished for a different position, one that wouldn't have him see the weight of the UN's judgment on his every action, and under Morrison he would get his wish, as he would get to coordinate a small team to cover operations.

Under Morrison's command, Overwatch was set to help rebuild the world, and as a statement of their newfound purpose, the first new addition to the Strike team since the Crisis ended would be announced. Emre Sarioglu, a tactical genius from Turkey who had grown in notoriety for both his military prowess, and his innate ability to build connections with everyone he worked for. While the rest of the Strike team often seemed untouchable, mighty and superhuman, Emre was just a regular guy, who always kept fighting a good fight, and inspiring others to do the same. He would go on to become an invaluable asset and a great middle man between the Strike team and the supporting staff across all of Overwatch's divisions, especially Search and Rescue.

events.name: Operation White Dome
events.description:
Not so long after Sarioglu's recruitment, he would be deployed in Istabul for Operation White Dome. The team led by Ana Amari were meant to quell a pocket of hostile Anubis loyal Omnics. Neither Reyes or Morrison participated. As a way to show the world, Overwatch had the resources to match curated teams for different missions, but this ultimately put extra pressure on the Strike team, as they would be ambushed and take heavy fire that resulted in Lindholm being critically injured, causing him to lose both an arm and an eye. Wilhelm sustained lesser injuries while trying to stabilize Torbjörn.

The mission was a success, but it highlighted that the Strike team was in need of more recruits, and with the blessing of the UN, Morrison began expansion across the entire organization.

Overwatch agent severely wounded

events.name: Brigitte Lindholm is Born
events.description:
On September 22nd of 2053, Brigitte Lindholm was born. The youngest of Torbjörn and Ingrid's children. Following Operation White Dome, Reinhardt saw to Lindholm's recovery at the hospital. The grumpy old man grew frustrated by his insistence, so he proposed that if he left him alone, he would allow him to name his soon-to-be-born daughter. Reinhardt agreed, choosing the name Brigitte. The pair would grow close following the mission, and Lindholm would ultimately choose Wilhelm as the child's godfather, making him part of the family.

Brigitte was the first of Torbjörn's kids to show interest in engineering. Following her father's footsteps by tinkering in his workshop, and listening to Reinhardt's chivalry stories when he paid them a visit.

events.name: Australian Liberation
events.description:
After the crisis, most Omnium factories remained mostly intact, quarantined from Anubis' grasp, so most governors elected to keep the facilities around for study and potentially to find a better use for them. Some countries instead chose to leave them untouched, to serve as cultural landmarks of Omnic culture, though even among Omnics this idea was heavily debated with mixed results. In reality, there was a bigger reason why the Omniums remained standing after the Crisis, and that was the dangerous fusion reactors deep in their core.

In Australia, the government chose to lend the Outback Omnium and the surrounding land to the Omnics, as a peace offering. But in doing so, this displaced many indigenous residents, rural communities, solar farmers and survivalists who fought hard during the crisis to keep their homes. Their protests over the decision would go unheard, and lead to the creation of the Australian Liberation Front, an extremist group that sought the complete removal of the Omnic presence from their nation. The ALF grew quickly and caught Overwatch's eyes, but while they monitored them closely, what happened next took everyone by surprise.

Members of the ALF manage to break into the Omnium and, in their attempt to seize it, sabotage the fusion core, resulting in a nuclear explosion visible throughout the entirety of Australia, turning the Outback into an irritated wasteland and decimating the Omnic population beyond repair, leaving the survivors to turn feral from the harsh conditions of the Outback. The ALF settled themselves in the ruins of the Omnium, and established an unofficial Monarchy in what would become Junkertown. Overwatch was about to intervene when the government determined the situation to be a lost cause and a public relations nightmare. Overwatch stood down, and the Junkers remained, though closely monitored, living miserable lives in an apocalypse of their own making.
headlines: ALF extremist group causes nuclear fallout in the Outback
Tensions between humans and Omnics intensify in Australia

events.name: Watching the Globe
events.description:
As Overwatch expanded its personnel, they found themselves in need for more bases of operations. The solution became the Watchpoint system, massive specialized facilities all across the world that would not only allow Overwatch to focus its dedicated personnel into a single place, but also increase their influence and ease of action in many different countries. Plenty of criticism rose from the UN, with many thinking it was an overreach, but the approval rates were overwhelming, as after all, who wouldn't want superheroes living in their neighborhood? With the rise of Omnic violence, the many cities in need of humanitarian aid and rebuilding efforts being halted by lack of manpower, Overwatch's help was welcomed all around the globe.

Acquiring permission for building the Watchpoints was a political battle of its own, but some of them came from resources shared from within. The Martins had been given a small military outpost and research lab on the Strait of Gibraltar following their support of Spain during the Crisis. Once the war was over, the base went unused, so they decided to donate the outpost to become the very first Watchpoint. Many bases were built for specialized purposes. Gibraltar got turned into an Orbital Launch site, but in addition to that, Overwatch would soon see many other bases. Grand Mesa, a weapons development facility. Kerguelen, reserved for biological studies. Xining, focused on social analysis and Omnic integration, as well as later on the maximum security prison at Grímsvötn. In addition to the Watchpoints, Overwatch also established headquarters in Zurich, establishing the beginning of a new hopeful era for humanity.

events.name: One with the Iris
events.description:
The newly awakened Omnics found themselves in a cold, hostage world. Many were persecuted if not outright hunted and killed, yet on top of it all, most ended up questioning the reason for their existance. How did they cope with being brought to life in such dire conditions? These Omnics traveled the world looking for answers, just like Aurora once did, and it wouldn't be long before they began following her path.

A cult of travelers led by Tekhartha Mondatta, who dedicated his life to studying Aurora's life, trying to gather a complete picture to truly understand her choice, and his place in the world. His journey took the Omnics to Suravasa, where they would choose to abandon the secular world, and begin Aurora's pilgrimage into the Nepalese Himalayas, to honor her memory. Their journey would take the monks to the ruins of a monastery, Aurora's resting place, where she had finally found enlightenment. In there laid the Iris, a metaphysical anomaly formed as a result of the Awakening pulse. The Monks would mark the start of the Shambali religion, studying the Iris, harnessing its energy in hand-crafted orbs to use for meditation, and living under the ideal that unity and coexistence between Humans and Omnics was achievable, as the way they saw it, they are all one within the Iris.

It would be here where two Omnics, Ramattra and Zenyatta, would become as close as brothers. Ramattra had chosen to follow Mondatta, lost in grief by the suffering of his people. He later invited Zenyatta to join him, as he hoped together they could find a sense of purpose and harmony amidst conflict. The Shambali faith would quickly grow across the Omnic communities of the world and be a key factor in the Omnic rights movement. Dr. Mina Liao herself would often visit the Monastery, both for study of the Iris, and to organize meetings amongst leaders, like Mondatta, as she had grown to be quite the prominent Omnic right activist following the Awakening. The monks naturally welcomed her warmly, and shared her cause.
headlines: Meet the Shamblai, the new Omnic Religion
Omnics across the world pilgrim towards the Himalayas

events.name: Anima Weaponry
events.description:
The ancient Shimada clan remained strong even after the crisis. A family of criminals that had a choke hold over Tokyo, but unlike many of the rival clans, they had gained the trust and even loyalty of the locals, seeing themselves as rulers and ruled. As such, inspired by their shady nature, the people of the Kanezaka region respected the clan as protectors, and the Shimada in turn kept opposing clans away, preventing them from hurting their people.

A big part of the Shimada's success was owned by the blacksmith Toshiro Yamagami, creator of the Anima blades. Toshiro was a weapon smith in possession of unique avatar technology, capable of imbuing traditional weaponry with an ethereal, malleable energy. These weapons were not only enhanced by the Anima's energy, but with enough discipline, their users would be able to manifest projections spirits at will. Toshiro would serve the clan, designing blades for their family, which in turn used them to suppress the opposition and solidify their cultural position as the rulers of the region.

While the clan forbid Toshiro from making any Anima Avatars after forging dragon blades for the Shimada heirs, Toshiro had one last masterpiece that he kept a secret from the clan. The man had planned to propose to what would be his wife, Asa Yamagami, a skilled sword master, daughter to the shrine maiden of the local Fox shrine. To win her mother's heart, Toshiro crafted Ofuda Talismans imbued with Anima technology, which would bring the Fox spirit to life, and assist the old lady in her Miko duties.
headlines: Gang violence in Japa slows down, but Shimada activity is at an all time high
Shimada Clan operators wield unknown energy swords during confrontation with rival groups

events.name: Undercover
events.description:
While Morrison had his hands full, expanding across the globe, Reyes was beginning to set up his Blackwatch Division, a small team for covert operations that would allow him to pursue justice outside red tape and protocol. Gérard Lacroix, from the administrative and diplomatic division, would come in to fill the role as Reyes's primary partner. A Parisian diplomat with extensive intelligence work, which quickly drew him into Reyes's orbit. Lacroix himself was recently married to a renowned ballerina, Amélie Lacroix, who gave little thought of her husband's career, as it mostly made for a conversation topic at parties in her eyes.

Together, Reyes and Lacroix would prove the merits of Blackwatch and quickly begin a take-down campaign on crime and violence across the world.

events.name: Miracle Worker
events.description:
Like many others around the world, young Angela Zeigler had lost her parents to the Crisis, while they volunteered at a local hospital. This traumatic event would serve to keep her focus on her studies. The girl would go on to become one of the youngest graduates of the medical program at the University of Zurich and quickly rose up to be quite the name in the scientific world, meeting brilliant minds like Siebren de Kuiper while at school. She would later enroll at Chalmers University in Gothenburg, where she focused on nanobiology and developed several breakthroughs in the field and its uses in medicinal science.

Angela had visited Torbjörn at the hospital following his injury during Operation White Dome, as her family were close friends with the Linholms. During the visit, he was so impressed by her papers on nanobiotics that he sent a recommendation to Morrison to fund her schooling and offer monetary support. On Linholm's advice, Angela had also begun volunteering for Overwatch's medical staff, becoming one of very few civilians allowed to tour Watchpoints. With their funding and guidance, Zeigler was a brilliant mind with an even brighter future lying ahead of her.

Following the Omnic Crisis, most SEP subjects chose to lie low and fade into private lives, Soldier 00 included. Sierra was unknowingly the daughter of a super soldier, and seemingly inherited her mother's gifts. Sadly, however, this soon enough would put a target on both of their backs.

events.name: Deadlock Rebels
events.description:
Years of neglect led Ashe to constantly breaking the law, and gaining a reputation with the authorities. On one occasion, she was arrested for armed assault inspired by it being in self-defense. While spending time in jail, waiting to be bailed out, she met Cole Cassidy in the cell block. Like many others around the area, he was employed by nearby Megafarms after they lost their Omnic staff. The farms were filled with refugees during the war, and eventually they came to be used for cheap labor. Cass had ended up locked after a brawl broke out among the farmhands. Ashe came to envy her new friend, for the freedom he had as a ruffian, drifting away without having to answer to anybody.

After the bailout, Ashe was greeted by disapproving parents on a holoscreen, not even trying to listen to her side of the story. They had grown tired of her actions and decided that once she turned 18, they would cut off from the family and its finances. In a fit of anger, Ashe plotted how to get back at her family for how they treated her, and realized selling Arbalest goods to the black market would be the best way to bite back, but in order to do so she needed to pull a heist. Ashe and B.O.B would then drive by the Ace Valentine megafarm to pick up Cassidy, and together they started to assemble a team to steal from the Arbalest facilities her family controlled. A fellow farmhand,of Cass, Julian and a hired hacker going by Frankie would join their ranks, forming the start of the Deadlock Rebels.

They were not the only ones with this daring ideals, the harsh life after the crisis had led to the rise of gang activity across the now called "New West", and as Deadlock expanded across the country they would often come into conflict with other gang territories. During a confrontation with the Diamond backs, Julian would end up betraying the group, getting Cassidy captured, but with some help on the inside from Benito Sanchez, Ashe and Frankie would be able to not just rescue Cassidy, but to overthrow Diamonback leader ship out of their den. The remainder gang members would choose to follow their new leader, as Ashe slowly begane to form a name for herself in the New West

Arbalest reports assets stolen after repeated heists

events.name: Rebuilding the Past
events.description:
A notable trend across the world following the Crisis was the return to classical architecture. As many cities had been leveled by the war, many of them needed to rebuild from the ground up, changing greatly from how they used to look years prior. With the construction of the Temple of Anubis, Cairo gained a new Pyramid. As Lumérico built power plants across Mexico, their architecture was inspired by Aztec and Mayan architecture. And this trend can be seen across the world on many other sites. In Greece, a small Aegean island renamed itself to Illios and built a giant statue on the nearby coast, echoing stories of the Colossus of Rhodes. In Iraq, the ever-growing spire of the Oasis University calls back to the tales of the Tower of Babel, a never-ending spear or information growing into the sky.

But perhaps none of these rebuilt structures stands out more than the Colosseo Gladiatori. Italy had rebuilt the Colosseum and reopened it for the masses. The newly formed Gladiator games were a pageant that became a cultural touchstone of Rome in the wake of the crisis. Within the Arena Victoriae of the Colosseo, all sorts of events would be hosted to entertain both the public and the European elite. From one-to-one gladiator matches, to robotic chariot races, mech fights and even stand-offs against machines designed for war, much like those Anubis used in The Crisis.

The Colosseo grew over the years, and the champions of the Arena would quickly become influential figures in Europe too, as the Elites visiting the Colosseo would often sponsor their fights and take in profits from their victories.

Raised to be the perfect prodigy, aspiring to transcend the sky-high expectations set by her parents, both of them Fire College graduates of Wuxing that commanded operations during the Crisis. At the age of three, they both personally trained her in academic and martial arts, enrolling her in competitive Wushu-taolu and classical dance classes as she grew older. Anran persevered through the pressure, training through injuries and breaking records every year, never finishing off the podium.

events.name: The New West
events.description:
The rise of Gang violence across the American Southwest quickly became a topic of interest for Overwatch, and when needed, they would address these gangs individually by dispatching agents, and later send the engineering teams for repairs. Among the gangs, the rising threat of the Deadlock Rebels became of special concern to Reyes. The leader, Calamity Ashe was fast at forming alliances with other rival gangs, and had created a stranglehold on the region. Reyes would take a small team of troops to investigate the situation personally. After gathering intelligence on the gang, a massive operation to take down the Rebels was put into action.

Cole Cassidy, a founding member of the gang, would create a distraction that resulted in his arrest, but allowed the rest of the leadership to escape. Ashe, Frankie and Bez would continue to search for him in hopes of rescue, but they did not know that it was too late for that, as Cassidy had been taken by Overwatch, not as a prisoner but as a willing recruit.

events.name: Outlaw
events.description:
After the Deadlock raid, Reyes surprised everyone when he brought their prisoner alongside him. Gabriel saw talent in Cassidy, notin his guts, loyalty and excellent marksmanship for someone his age. With enough guidance he could turn his life around, and Reyes believed it would be a waste to lock him up without a second change.


Thus he gave him the choice, to either rot in jail for years, or to come with him as his student, and make better use of his skill as an Overwatch agent. While many saw his inclusion as conscription of a minor, he seemed pleased with the arrangement and quickly proved himself to be an efficient asset. He would be placed under Ana's wing to hone his skills with the gun, and soon after, he would join Reyes in the Blackwatch division. As Reyes understudy, Cass would often think back of Deadlock, assuming he did the right thing because his gang never came  to his rescue, convincing himself that this path meant he was better than them, clueless of the fact that his old partners were still desperately trying to save him by the time he agreed to join Reyes. Still, Cassidy made for a great addition to the Strike team and a valuable asset for Reyes' division.

events.name: On the Run
events.description:
While the US publicly seemed OK with SEP soldiers vanishing into obscuring, something about Soldier: 00 appears to be different. Kendra Banks had been the first candidate for the Soldier Enhancement Program, and much like the rest of Project Naughton, her service was heavily redacted and poorly documented. What little is known suggests she defected from the program shortly after her procedure and has been in hiding ever since. Kendra hoped living in Appalachia would keep her and her family hidden, but only a year after her daughter Sierra was born, Kendra would be found.

Kendra Banks, now going by Kendra Woods, fled into the night and took Sierra with her, as government agents raided their house asking questions about Project Naughton to her husband, James Turner. Ever since that day, Sierra has lived on the run, bouncing from one school to another, completely unaware of her mother's past. Each time they were found, they would have to move out quickly, and to calm little Sierra down, Kendra knew how to make a game out of evacuation and protocol.

Through it all, Sierra lived a relatively normal life. While she was always the new kid and had little to no friends due to this, she always placed at the top of her class and demonstrated innate physical skills and sharp thinking. While she always attributed this to her mother's teachings, little did she know one of Doctor Naughton's main hypothesis with the Soldier Enhancement Program, was the possibility of children inheriting the modifications.
headlines: US Government publicly allows SEP Soldiers to remain anonymous

Her father was a former professional gamer during his youth and he would bond with his daughter through the hobby, playing early century classics like StarCraft with her, which quickly made it apparent Hana had excellent skills, just like her father.

Hana would grow up to be a heavily competitive woman, staying up late with her childhood friend Dae-Hyun to work on his hover bike. She would often race and on one occasion she managed to win, at the cost of the bike and nearly taking her own life due to the reactor overloading.

events.description: On April 9th of 2057, Mizuki Kawano was born in Fukuoka, under the shadow of misfortune. Ever since he was a baby, his family had been surrounded by unexplained tragedy. His grandparents suddenly lost to unnatural causes, and years later his mom would drown in a freak accident. His father, quite the superstitious man, was quick to assume the bloodline must have been cursed, and from there Mizuki's life would only continue to go downhill. As the mental state of his dad worsened, he ingrained the idea of a curse in the poor kid's head, telling him tales of all the malevolent spirits that haunted the family ever since he was born.

events.name: Vancouver Floods
events.description:
Overwatch aided in the rebuilding efforts all around the world. In the words of Torbjörn himself, they were "The Janitors of the Apocalypse", and their efforts became invaluable in getting the world back together.

The engineering team was working on repairing the Canadian pipeline system when a massive storm struck, causing much of British Columbia to flood all the way down to Seattle.

Overwatch's quick response allowed the displaced locals to survive the disaster, as both a field team and support staff were sent to assist in the evacuation of both people and wildlife. Thanks to Overwatch the situation was dealt with swiftly and with minimal losses. Once the water receded, Amari coordinated with local authorities to distribute resources with the refugees, while Lindholm's team began the construction of a series of Dams and bridges across the West Coast, to reroute water out to the sea.

The Vancouver Flood response made headlines and many proved Overwatch's worth in a post-crisis world, showing that, in spite of their superhuman capabilities, they were also more than willing to come down and help with the dirty work.

events.name: Building Coalitions
events.description:
As Overwatch expanded and aided the world heal, they sought to form good relationships with other peacekeeping operations around the globe, particularly those that didnt make a part of the original recruitment process during the Crisis. Just like how Doomfist chose to protect his own home over joining Overwatch, many other groups like the Inti Warriors of Peru or the Caribbean Coaliton rejected Adawe's proposal, oftne due to intense regional pressure and concerns joining Overwatch would leave their home undefended. Morrison outreach groups all over the world, from Peru to Polynesia, Jordan and Bangladesh to try and collaborate in the post-Crisis landscape, but one of these partners proved to be closer than the rest.

The Caribbean Coaltion remained strong even as the Crisis ended, but it quickly fueled concerns among the governments of the Caribbean and Latin America that the alliance defied their sovereignty. This was part of a global wave of nationalism following the Crisis, as many nations, struck by years of violence and destruction, chose to spend resources inward. The Coaltion defied that mindset, and understood that without sharing their resources in these dire times the Caribbean would never bounce back. The Concerned Nations requested Overwatch to intervene and dismantle the rouge Coalition at once, but to everyone's surprise, Commander Morrison chose to aid the Coalition's efforts instead, deploying the strike team and much of the medical crew, including Angela Ziegler herself. To the people of the Caribbean, Overwatch were more than welcome, and a wave of recruitments soon followed, but the governments soon would come to resent this response and in the years to follow they would be less inclined to speak positively of Overwatch.

During this period, a young orphan, Jean-Baptiste Augustin, would await the day he could enlist into Overwatch, hoping he could be a medic alongside his heroes. However, as the governments resisted their presence, Overwatch recruitment would never come to Haiti, so without many other options to survive, Baptiste chose to join the military, working as a combat medic for the Caribbean Coalition, hoping one day he could be like the heroes from the posters.

Kiriko grew to be a very idealistic, impressionable kid who did not quite fully understand the reality of her situation, of the clan brawls that plagued Kanezaka, and the role her family played in endorsing them, but despite of that, her idealistic self and the teachings of her grandma's faith pushed her to help others and follow her own path.

events.name: The Architech Academy
events.description:
In the years that followed the Crisis, Vishkar would quickly rise into a global powerhouse. Having helped the Indian government during the war, hard light technology advanced greatly in the span of a few years, and with the world left in ruins, the company saw it as a great chance to spearhead the reparations. But in spite of their power, they had far too few hands. Most of the early architects had either left the company years ago or died during the Crisis. Hard light technology was quite complex, and it required years of training to master, but in looking for solutions to their shortage of staff, Vishkar would make the controversial decision to turn to the hundreds of orphans left by the crisis.

With the approval of a desperate government, Vishkar would open the Architect Academy, a school meant to pipeline kids across the nation into becoming the architects of the future. Staffed with the best professionals in the country and providing housing and education to any orphan they could find. To pay for their education, the students would merely be required to complete a five-year tour of duty for the company following their graduation day, which would in most cases set them off for success and open doors within the corporation. While initially met with outcry about the coercing of minors, the Academy was a success in the long run, which led to many wealthy families across the globe sending their children to the Academy, with fully paid tuition to avoid the post-graduation requirements. It is here where Satya Vaswani and Niran Pruksamanee first crossed paths, the son of a wealthy family and a girl plucked out of poverty, spending their student days as roommates and quickly gaining notoriety amongst their peers for their unique perspective and techniques.

events.name: Liao's Renaissance
events.description:
With the Crisis, the world had lost faith in Artificial Intelligence all together. Between heavy tensions with Omnics growing worldwide and the repurposing, if not outright, shutdown of God programs worldwide, the planet was ready to move on from AI, but Mina Liao was eager to revitalize the world's trust in her field. Years after the Crisis, Dr. Liao would reveal her first major project under Overwatch, the Athena artificial intelligence system.

In spite of taking the name of a deity, Athena was not a full god program in the traditional sense. In spite of the limited scope of the program's intelligence, Athena was able to optimize missions, providing detailed briefings and projected areas of potential conflict, way before any agents received intel on a task. The assistant proved successful in many missions and paved the road for further projects under Liao's guidance, albeit without truly removing safe lines around her research.

Liao still grieved the loss of Aurora as her creator. In her eyes, the Omnics were Aurora's legacy, and she hoped the for equality between Humans and Omnics could one day be achieved. While she was not often a public speaker, her writings and interviews quickly positioned her as a powerful ally to the rising Omnic rights movement, for which she later joined in a handful of rallies and marches.

events.name: Gwishin
events.description:
Years after the end of the Crisis a Robotic Colossus emerged from the East China Sea, immediately making its advance towards South Korea and setting off Overwatch's alarms. Morrison recalled all agent fields and directed every resource they had towards the machine. The battle against the colossus was unlike any other; The robot dwarfed even the largest Crisis-era Titan units. By the time Overwatch and the Korean army, the Colossus was miles offshore, intercepting shipping vessels and destroying them without hesitation. Overwatch, not used to fighting in water, struggled to contain the beast and stop the violence, but in the end the Colossus was beaten back and presumably destroyed.

Some strike team members welcomed the battle as a chance to return to what they did best, while for many others this was far more concerning. Liao noticed the warbot's design felt unusual to her, so she requested a long-term field assignment to South Korea to study the remains, and while Korea had shown significant push back at the idea of establishing a Watchpoint in the country, they at least aided Liao by providing her with recovered pieces for Analysis. The primary concern was for some hostile group to have access to an unknown Omnium and use it to attack enemy nations. For the time being, it appeared there would be no way to find out what happened, and Liao's research would not yield any results for months. The beast would be known as the Gwishin, a type of spirit curse to roam the earth in Korean folklore, a fitting name for a lingering ghost of the Omnic Crisis.

Seeing as Korea would not allow the construction of an base, Overwatch would establish Watchpoint: Xining in China. Originally planed as a Busan base before Korea's rejection, the base  would focus on analyzing world politics and inter personal patterns, as well as working towards Omnic Integration and Crisis recovery of communities around Asia. However te base would also act as Overwatch's nearest deployment in case of another Gwishin attack.

events.name: Ecological Initiative
events.description:
With God programs being gone, the responsibility of their duties fell back on humanity once more, and while in a lot of cases their functionality was rerouted or essentially taken over by other organizations, in some cases, such as environmental duties, they started to become heavily neglected. Anubis was meant to be used for ecological preservation, and yet with most infrastructure lost, the whole left by the program began to become apparent to Hector and Claudio. The Martins were long time environmentalist advocates, and they believed that Overwatch could be used to improve the world, so they took a proposal to Morrison and the UN to create a specialized Watchpoint branch. The Ecopoint Program would create stations staffed with Overwatch's scientific leads in critical locations across the globe, where they could monitor ongoing abnormalities in nature and aid in global efforts for preservation of the environment. In spite of a wave of nationalism and distrust for Overwatch presence hitting many countries over the years, the Ecopoints were quite a welcomed proposal, as the Martins were highly respected names in the international communities they worked with, and the benefits of scientifically-oriented bases were far more self-evident than the more generalist and often military nature of Watchpoints. With the approval of the program, other big names like Mui and Namuaca quickly joined, adding further credibility to the initiative. 

In the end, seven initial bases were approved, with more coming later. Ecopoint Antartica focused on monitoring the impacts of climate change and pursuing methods of recovery of both the atmosphere and the ice caps, as well as studying strange fluctuations found around the Peninsula. Samoa would receive a base within one of the active volcanoes, to focus on seismic monitoring and geothermal research, detecting multiple major earthquakes in the region and saving countless lives. Ecopoint Connemara focused on the restoration of Irish boglands, later on collaborating with Russian scientists to aid in revitalizing the Siberian lowlands. Ecopoint Osa in Costa Rica would focus on the recovery of reptiles and amphibians of the Corcovado cloud forest, seen as a bellwether of the rest of the natural world, focusing primarily on antifungals and biological solutions to waste treatment. Ecopoint Zelandia off the coast of New Zeland would exist in response to the Australian fall-out zone, focusing on filtering radiation and aiding in the recovery of Oceania following the ALF's attack. Ecopoint Tarapoto, deep within the Colombian Amazon, was built to study the many ecosystems of the area. And lastly, Ecopoint Monterrey, a submarine base off the shore of California, meant to research marine life and follow protocols originally proposed by Leon Honisch to aid in the recovery of many species. The program was overall seen as one of Overwatch's most clean-cut successes and net positives to society at large, and a great example of the good they could do for the world

events.name: The Scourge of Numbani
events.description:
The City of Harmony, Numbani would see ongoing expansion for years following Adawe's departure, to great success and reception worldwide. The City State emerged in Nigeria under Adawe's vision, to build a place for people of all kinds, human and Omnics, to live in unity. In order to achieve this, she collaborated with an Omnic task force that would pioneer what a truly Omnic-inclusive environment looked like, from tailored housing that accommodated their needs instead of human ones, lacking kitchens and instead hosting things like oiling gear. Clinics that performed maintenance and repair of crisis wounds, psychological services to help with the trauma that followed the war and the trouble of consciousness, as well as spiritual centers for study of philosophy and theology, especially with the rising Shambali faith. A lot of focus was also placed on giving Omnics resources to find jobs outside their pre-Crisis designation, letting them pursue their own dreams outside human expectations and design. 

The push for Omnic infrastructure would lead to heavy investment in intellectual and artistic enrichment, which would lead to a thriving sense of pride and community for the arts in the city. A lot of the principles used when building Numbani were modeled after the infrastructure and plans of the new Toronto, following their own Omnic Integration. By the time the city was finished, it had proved to the world that coexistence was possible, and the city became a beacon of hope for Omnic allies around the globe. The day the last brick was laid would be declared Unity Day, and celebrated as a holiday in the city to mark the huge accomplishment of Omnic and Human Collaboration. Shortly after the inauguration, Adawe passed away, and was mourned by the people across the entire city, and celebrated as a Nigerian hero, not unlike Adhabu Ngumi, the first Doomfist.

The war hero had been buried with his weapon, and celebrated across the nation, until one day a man appeared in Numbani wielding an upgraded gauntlet, stolen from the grave. Akinjide Adeyemi, a warmonger who profiteered from constant raids in the city, declaring it a blight upon Nigeria and swearing to destroy it. Known as the Scourge of Numbani, between the gauntlet and a small militia distributed across Africa he became, this second Doomfist would terrorize the city for nearly twelve years, and since Overwatch lacked jurisdiction in Numbani, they were unable to intervene from the inside, but they found ways around it. While Scouting for Ecopoint locations, many other Watchpoints would get approved, such as Xining and Grimsvotn, but during this process Ana Amari noted that among the many bases, Overwatch had a shamefully thin presence around Central Africa, and under her guidance Watchpoint: Kisangani got approved. While the base focused on informatics security and computer science, it would enable Overwatch to respond to threats around the region, fighting Doomfist and his lieutenants from the outside, primarily through the efforts of a new Strike team member. Wildebeest.

events.name: The Red Promise
events.description:
The Horizon Lunar Colony had been a long-lasting success by this point, but Lucheng Interstellar was never aiming for the Moon. Their eyes always lie beyond the stars. So many years after the Moon base's establishment, the company felt ready to begin the process on the next frontier: Mars.

The initial proposal, however, was denied by the UN, but this did not stop Lucheng. They would keep the plans in motion funded internally, and begin gathering a group of scientists and engineers in the public eye. The small team was going to be the first phase of Project: Red Promise, a multi-stage terraforming Initiative to colonize the surface of Mars. At first, the project carried on smoothly, as the astronauts arrived on the red planet's surface and established a base within a matter of months. What none of them expected was that two of the team members, Dr. Jiayi Teo and Dr. Khiem Van Minh, were having a baby

events.name: Omnic Rights
events.description:
The decade following the crisis saw the rise of the Omnic Rights movement, as it became clear to the world as a whole that the sentience was permanent and impossible to ignore, and Omnics themselves grappled with their place in the world. While cities like Toronto and Numbani made active efforts towards Omnic integration, the reality is that the world at large was generally far more hostile. From the cleansing of Ravager units, to the destruction of the Australian Omnium, or the inhospitable conditions of the London Underworld, to domestic units being thrown into the streets all over the planet, as their previous families rejected them or in worse cases, were forced back into servitude. Something needed to change, and here, so an organized movement began to campaign for the United Nations, seeking for global recognition of rights and obligations for all Omnics, to be treated with equal respect and empathy as humans.

When the UN finally took a stance on the issue, Liao was called as a witness for the case of Omnic sentience. In the hearings she brought in the research and records she had performed while under Ovewatch, alongside previous evidence used for Aurora's original case. In response to the mountains of undeniable evidence, the UN agreed and granted Omnic kind legal personhood status worldwide. In spite of this, not every government complied, and many humans would continue to resent and discriminate Omnic communities. Cases like Cliff Brassey, the first human charged for Omnic Murder, echo the feelings of many people around the world: "Breaking a machine is property damage, isn't it? This'll blow over. We'll put the tin cans back in the kitchen and forget this ever happened."
The road towards equality would be a long and dragged out one, but progress was possible, and more than welcomed.

events.name: Black Ops
events.description:
While Overwatch's reputation and support was still high across the world, over the last couple of of years they have experienced a series of rejections. Korea refused the establishment of a Watchpoint in Busan, then Numbani chose to remain outside of jurisdiction in spite of Doomfist's attacks. And many other things got in the way of operations around the world.

Reyes and Cassidy would begin a crackdown on criminals in Calgary, Chicago and Saint Paul. And during this time, a drug trafficker would raise a lawsuit in his defense, arguing Overwatch had no authority over living humans, only Omnics. Naturally, this was quickly dismissed and seen as an attempt to extort money and avoid prosecution, but the red tape and politics of it all slowed Overwatch down. After the crisis, a mission came up that would allow Morrison to put Overwatch's charter to a test. The family of an American diplomat had been kidnapped and taken into Russia, and Morrison tasked Amari to retrieve her. Ana sidestepped protocol and did the mission before Russia approved of her clearance. Everything went well. She tracked down the terrorist and retrieved the civilians alive, but in the end, she was intercepted by the Russian government and charged with illegal entering and obstruction of local law enforcement. Ana was released, but permanently banned from Russia.

This would not be the only time Overwatch broke protocol. An incident in Cape Town led to a rouge god program to escape containment and rebel against its captors, catching the attention of Anubis loyalists and forming an insurrection, but the red tape stopped Morrison from sending in any troops. Lucky for him, Lacroix had extensive covert ops training and came up with the idea of sending both him and Reyes "on vacation." The pair had a quiet trip, limiting themselves to intel gathering and evacuation of sources, as well as, on the rare occasion, the disappearance of one or two criminals. Coincidentally, their vacation led to the apprehension of the rouge Ai and the loyalist suddenly vanished, all without official Overwatch involvement. Following this success and the repeated tension with international bureaucracy, Morrison would approve of more and more vacations between the Blackwatch staff, Cassidy joining them in many cases, but whether Jack knew the full extent of these trips is unknown.

events.name: Growing with the Clan
events.description:
Over the years, the Shimada family had come to rely on the Yamagami Blades, the business run by Toshiro and his wife, Asa. The Yamagami name has served the city for generations following traditional paths, heavily linked by the local shrine to the Fox Spirit, and now under Shimada control, the family chose their side in the turf war of the clans. Not only did Toshiro develop the Anima weapons used by the clean head and his sons, but his wife proved to be quite an asset as well. Often regarded as the best sword master in the world, Asa would serve as the teacher to the Shimada brothers. From a very young age, Asa wished for his daughter to also choose the path of the blade, so since she was very little, Kiriko would often join the brothers in training. Both brothers would quickly grow attached to the little girl, seeing her as a little niece figure. Genji in particular, was one to slack off from training and play with the kids, stealing sweets from the kitchen and playing games in the arcade.

 It was not just the brothers who Kiriko grew close to, since she spent so much time in the Shimada Castle, the clan leader, Sojiro Shimada had also grown accustomed to her presence. She would often join the family during the fireworks festivals at the end of summer, and during one night, he told his sons the stories of the clan. How centuries ago, a rival clan attempted to end their rule by setting the orchards aflame, but the Shimada did not move to defend the fields. Instead, they aided the people. These teachings heavily shaped Kiriko's perspective of the clan as Kanezaka's protectors, while she was still too young to understand the true nature of their operations.

On the off days when Kiriko was not training, she spent time at the Fox shrine with her grandmother, who taught her the traditional ways of spiritual cleansing and meditation. From an early age, her grandmother would teach him how to use the Anima ofuda that her dad had crafted, to summon the Fox spirit and use it to aid those in need. Much like how her mother wanted her to follow in her footsteps, her grandma had laid another path for her as well.

events.name: Survivor
events.description:
"Dez" Stone would grow up in the living hell of the Australian Outback, where other exhiled savages roamed in caravans fighting in an unending battle for land and resources, in a hostile, radiated wasteland. If that was not enough already, wastlanders still needed to defend themselves from the Omnics, who had been forever changed by the radiation messing with their systems and the struggle to survive in harsh environments pushed them to become feral. Neither machines nor men were truly sane or safe. The Stones fought hard to survive this harsh life but one by one, they would fall all of them, except for Dez. Fueled by her anger towards King Mason for doing this to her family, and the loss of her loved ones, Odessa endured for years and became a formidable wasteland warrior, who scrapped Feral Omnics for parts to use on armor and weapons, including an electro magnetic gauntlet that proved deadly against anyone who opposed her. 

Years later, the memories of that day, where her family had vanished, still cause her nightmares.

events.name: Pocket King
events.description:
Following the Crisis many Omnics struggled to integrate into human society. Very few of them managed to climb up the ladder of capitalism, but perhaps there is no bigger success than that of Maximillien. Before the crisis, Max was a low-level accountant in Marseille, but following the Awakening he found himself in the position of a preeminent financial advisor for La Coterie, the name the press had given to a group of Europe's most influential power brokers. While he was often stepped on by many of his clients, soon enough they all began kneeling at his feet.  For the longest time, his clients blindly trusted their assets into Max, and behind their backs he had built an elaborate web of money laundering and contingency agreements, very carefully worded deals that would all direct those fortunes directly to him. After years of serving ignorant, arrogant Tycoons, Max arranged a meeting with the six most powerful figures of Monaco, and broke to them the news; he owned each one of them and their companies. 

Overnight, Max had become one of the most powerful figures in out of Europe, and the head of La Coterie. The Omnic was one call away from bankrupting anyone he wished to, so his old clients had no real choice but to serve him. Over the years, Maximillien had guided the reconstruction and rise of Monte Carlo from the shadows, and after years of effort he owned half of the city. His reign over Monaco would continue to expand as he pursued other deals across Europe and beyond, later acquiring Don Rumbotico in Cuba and using it as yet another front for his money laundering. From the window views of his casino, Max watched as the world's wealthiest and powerful elites fed from his hand.

events.name: Juno Teo Minh is Born
events.description:
On Marsh 22nd of the year 2060, Juno Teo Minh, the first Martian, was born. Juno was a surprise to the Red promise team, but in a lot of ways she embodied the hopes of the project, and everyone loved her as if she was their own kid. While the scientists were often busy with their duties at the base, they made an active effort to give Juno a happy and healthy childhood, but it was clear their curiosity rubbed off on the young girl. Having been born in an early colony with so few people, in a world so far away from Earth, Juno was quite sheltered and inexperienced with a lot of the ways the world worked, things that would be normal to an Earth-born human were completely alien and often terrifying for her.

It also meant that Juno was always eager to get out there, and see more of her home. She wanted to help the team join in expeditions on the planet's surface, and once she was old enough, they would provide her with her own space suit, allowing her to roam through the outdoors of the colony and aid the scientists on the staff, as a true member of the team. Still, she often fantasized about Earth, especially when watching the Cowboy movies that her dad had brought with him.

events.name: Valkirie
events.description:
It had been ten years since the end of the Crisis, and Angela's early career was already a fruitful one. Her breakthroughs in the field of applied nanobiology radically improved the treatment of life-threatening injuries, capable of stabilizing near-death patients and saving countless lives. Through her schooling and early years outside of college, Overwatch kept an eye on her, allowing her access to the Watchpoints as she volunteered on the medical staff. Ziegler's technology could have made her rich if she chose to remain in private practice, but she always saw science as something that belonged to the world and the people who benefited from it.

It was here when Morrison finally chose to approach her directly. As the world finally moved away from the crisis, Morrison wished to shift towards peacetime and civilian applications. The heavy investment in the support departments and Ecopoint bases was one of the early signs of this new mission, making the world a better place. As such, Morrison offered Angela to become the leader of the medical division, with all the money, resources and personnel she could ever dream of. Angela was intrigued by these promises, but she was greatly opposed to the militaristic nature of Overwatch. Even since losing her parents to the war, she did not have the greatest opinion on military peacekeeping.

In the end, she had agreed to the offer with one caveat. The same way Torbjörn requested back in the day, she wanted to become a part of the strike team, so she could keep a level of control over the use of her technology and allow her to see at work personally. Morrison agreed. In order to work on the frontlines, she began development of the Valkyrie swift-response suit, a personal aerial combat armor that came equipped with nanobiotic capabilities and assistive protocols. Ziegler quickly proved to be a surprisingly adept field agent, with a surgical tactic approach to changing situations. Between her efficiency and the design of her suit, she quickly gained notoriety among the strike team, earning her the call sign "Mercy". While she did not enjoy the way this framed her, she was always glad to use her technology for the greater good.

Wuyang grew up seeking to meet his family's standards and would often train alongside his older sister in martial arts. While Anran was always outstanding, yet pressured to keep an eye on her younger brother, Wuyang tended to fall behind his sister, though they both aimed towards following the family's tradition and eventually getting accepted into Wuxing's fire College.

events.name: Radiation Poisoning
events.description:
With Angela leading the medical division, Overwatch began campaigns around the world using Nanobiotics to heal those in need, and perhaps there was no other place that needed it the most than Australia. Following the explosion of the Outback Omnium, the high amount of radioactive material blasted into the air and water led to a catastrophic health crisis in the region. While Ecopoint Zealandia focused on helping the environment heal, the people of Australia needed help to get through the radiation poisoning faster. Cases escalated, and the cancer rate skyrocketed, and in spite of Australia's prior resistance to Overwatch action, Ziegler would not stand by such a tragedy.

Under Angela's command, a mission team would be deployed, comprised of nearly the entire medical staff, alongside Chase and Sarioglu, both rising stars in the Strike team, well versed in public relationships. Together, they collaborated with local governments and local nonprofit groups to help get resources to affected populations, and bring those in remote locations into the Melbourne hospital, where Ziegler could give intensive care. Thanks to their efforts, the projected mortality rate was cut by more than seventy percent, and a wing of the Melbourne hospital was renamed for Ziegler. As the team was ready to leave, a group of Junkers came out of the You Yang Mountains and attempted to claim the city of Little River, hoping to take the medical facilities for themselves once Overwatch left. Ziegler and Chase were still present when the attack happened and were able to repel the raiders with no lives lost. However, they chose to stay at their post and monitor for further hostilities, delaying their return for over a month.

events.name: When you Wish
events.description:
Upon returning from Australia, Vivian would be surprised when she was dispatched to a smaller, though not less vital mission. She had been requested by a charity organization dedicated to creating memories for children in critical condition. Jada Levine, a twelve-year-old girl with a more aggressive case of the disease Vivian once had faced, was now moved to palliative care, and qualified for the organization's program. Her wish? You spend a day with Vivian Chase, the savior of Canada and her personal hero.

Vivian accepted, and two weeks later made the trip to Toronto, where the little kid was infatuated with Overwatch, and with Sojourn especially. The exact details of the meeting remain confidential and undocumented, except for Jada's own social media, filled with pictures of herself and her hero, during the last day of her life. Those pictures made the matter public, and Vivian came under fire for not bringing Mercy, the new team member hailed as a miracle worker, alongside her.  But Angela herself answered to this criticism, after getting permission from the parents to comment on her condition. The child was in a far too critical state, her condition too advanced, making surgeons unable to use cybernetics as an option to save her life. Nanobiotics as well had been taken to their extreme during the treatment. Even miracles have their limits. There was nothing Angela could have done, other than pull focus towards herself. Following this occasion, Chase became a far more vocal advocate for research into treatments for her disease, all the way through the end of her days in Overwatch.

events.name: Omnic Liberation
events.description:
While the Shambali faith aided hundreds of Omnics to find peace with themselves and the world, the threat of Omnic injustice still lingered, even way after the rulings in favor of personhood. Ramattra had found himself among the many monks who followed Tekhartha Mondatta's teachings, but he failed to see any progress in their vision. While the Shambali urged him to be patient, he could not stand the injustices that his people endured all around the world, without anyone taking action, and so on day, he decided to leave the temple, saying his farewells to his master, Mondatta and his closest friend, Zenyatta.

Ramattra wondered around the world and saw the horrors the Omnics lived through, not just discrimination and segregation, but many kidnapped and dismantled for parts, and those who lived relatively normal lives were forced into squalid conditions by ignorant humans who insisted they did not need sanitary measures, heat or water. In time, the brutal truth started to hit many Omnics. They were a one-generation species. Once they die, no more can be made. Their already limiting numbers were going down by resentful humans who could never understand what their lives were like, and so underground movements of angered Omnics began to appear. During his time in Europe, Ramattra would liberate many Omnics from forced servitude and begin to amass a following. While initially peaceful, this group stood in stark contrast to Mondatta's approach to coexistance; they were far more pro-active and resentful of humanity. This group would be known as Null Sector, a movement of Omnics that defy the numbers humans gave them, all zeroes, undefined.

events.name: Deep Rising
events.description:
Not long after the Gwishin attack, Overwatch received another emergency call from Busan. The Colossus had reappeared, larger than before and upgraded with new abilities. Overwatch dispatched all available agents, but even then, the tactics they used the previous time were no longer effective against it, and the team continued to struggle in maritime battles, so as the giant approached the coastline they were forced to evacuate the city. Fortunately, at the time, the strike team had grown rapidly, and new agents like Fusionator and Soundquake played a big role in defeating the Gwishin. The new agents had not faced the Gwishin before, yet their unique abilities proved effective when everything else failed. They were able to distract the colossus long enough for the rest of the team to take it down.

Mina Liao would be joined by Dr. Yi-am Lim, a robotics engineer from the Korean military. They both analyzed the footage and concluded the warbot was an upgraded version of that of the previous attack, but everyone wondered how that would be possible. It would require an entire team of the best engineers to get this machine running again after years, yet the timeline did not add up. Artificial Intelligence must have been involved in the accelerated development, and everyone began fearing the worst. Liao's research team was dispatched to the Temple of Anubis, to determine if the rouge AI had managed to escape containment, but they found no breaching happened. The researchers were astounded by trying to look for a source, when the third attack happened.

The colossus reemerged from the water, evolved yet again with a design that directly countered Soundquake's technology. Thanks to Dr Lim and yet another new agent, Rumble, the team was able to identify weak points and defeat the machine quicker, and with a smaller force this time. The swift response allowed Lim's team to retrieve the wreckage before the warbot dived back into the water. Thanks to this, they discovered the Gwishin had not been built in any known Omnium. Overwatch scouted the local sea floor and found a series of small drones lurking in the depths, attacking the diving team and forcing them to retreat. The only logical conclusion Liao could reach was that somewhere in the depths of the East China Sea, there was a secret Omnium, likely built by Anubis, and possibly it was not the only one. In spite of their combined efforts and Lim's wishes, Korea chose to remain independent in this battle, and Overwatch left after sharing their findings, despite Liao's frustration with the government choosing politics over allowing Overwatch to protect their people.

events.name: Specimen 8 is Born
events.description:
Lucheng's experiments with the Ape Colony had turned out fairly successful, and as the years went on, the animals began to develop unexpected side effects. Most notably, they grew in size and brain function. The team down on Earth was thrilled by the discovery and wished to experiment further to study any findings, but because of the funding of the Red Promise project, an already dangerous secret of theirs, spending more on such a morally questionable idea was risky, and far too expensive. To bypass the budget and moral concerns and keep the studies under the radar, Lucheng approved the use of hamsters as cheaper dispensable alternatives.

On October 15 of 2062, Specimen 8 would be born, and quickly experimented on like the rest of his siblings. Nicknamed "Hammond" by the researchers, he would quickly grow and surpass all their expectations of intelligence, completely overshadowing the rest of his siblings. Hammond to this day has memories of those early days of lonely enlightenment. The way his increased intelligence made it unable for his mother to communicate with him, how the scientists would bet on each of his siblings during every test, and how he hoped every time at least one of them would finally break through and join him. A painful existence under thoughtless Gods, where all he wished to do was escape.

events.name: Omnic Housing
events.description:
Just like his student before him, Tekhartha Mondatta would choose to leave the monastery not long after Ramattra did. While Ramattra sought to unify Omnics within a banner of justice and liberation, Mondatta believed the Shambali teachings could be used to teach equality and aid Omnic communities around the world through peaceful dialogue. In order to do this, Mondatta traveled the world, giving speeches and meeting with local authorities to discuss measures towards full Omnic Integration, and the place Mondatta had his eyes on to achieve the first milestone was King's Row, in London. 

The Omnics of England had been forced to live in the Underworld, a subterranean complex dug from abandoned Tube lines, power grid and sewage systems. Checkpoints around the city enforced curfews and police kept the Omnic people at bay to maintain segregation. This constant struggle had deemed London one of the main centers of Human Omnic tension in Europe, and the perfect spot for Mondatta to test his influence and teachings. Working with Major Mandah to begin work on Turing Green, a public housing development plan that would effectively give the Omnics a proper home, where they could live alongside human peers, without hiding underground. Mondatta's influence in the city could be felt within the Omnic community and with the groundbreaking of Turing Green, it marked the first step in rectifying years of injustice that Omnics had endured... if only it had lasted.

events.name: Harnessing te Harness
events.description:
Doctor De Kuiper had dedicated decades of his life to the study of gravitational forces and how to harness their power. His research had reshaped the world with flying cars and connecting the entire planet with a transcontinental train, and yet, he continues to seek more. Years of research in The Hague led him to believe it would soon be possible to harness the power of a black hole. In order to test his theory, he would perform an experiment within the Interstellar Journey Space Station. Then everything went wrong.

Something failed during the experiment, triggering the formation of a black hole for a brief, yet fatal moment. Siebren found himself right in the gravitational field of the experiment as the black hole appeared, and during this period of time he was exposed to its raw power. De Kuiper survived, both a miracle and a curse, as he now had to pay for the consequences of his hubris. The area nearing Dr. Siebren began to experience strange fluctuations in gravity, relative to his reactions to what was happening around him. To make matters worse, the experience left Siebren with serious psychological damage, incapable of acting coherently and mentioning a constant, maddening melody that nobody else heard. His distress caused the fluctuations to peak. He had to be evacuated immediately.

Upon returning to Earth, he would be quarantined in a Secret Government facility somewhere in New Mexico, raving about the true nature of the universe, incoherently talking about time being non-linear, all while gravity around him shifted out of control. Once the most celebrated scientists in human history, Siebren found himself unable to hold reality together, and was deemed a danger to himself, and the world as a whole. For years, he would be detained under the name of "Subject: Sigma", in an isolated room where his power could be contained. As years went on, the Doctor retreated into his own mind and the forbidden knowledge that had now cursed him was his only company. For years, he thought he would never see the outside world again.
