events.name: Pressure Makes a DIamond
events.description:
The Vishkar Empire was ever-growing, especially after the success of the Architech Academy. Although power stayed relatively within the family of the late founder, it would be more accurate to compare the company to a dynasty. Vaira Singhania was the granddaughter of Vishwakarma Bhatt himself, and as such she was expected to one day inherit the business for herself. From a very young age, she struggled to form coherent structures with Hard Light, mostly making organic, ill-defined shapes, and while she liked to consider them art, her family disagreed. All she saw from their eyes was disappointment, and this pressure quickly shaped her into a perfectionist, and someone very insecure about her own merits.

 As she grew older she developed a knack for corporate politics, excelling in all types of lessons from debates and economics, truly a woman shaped to lead a business as large as her family's. And yet, she still lacked the artistic eye needed to perfect Hard Light manipulation, a harsh truth that cut through her, especially with the opening of the academy. As young students graduated to become leading engineers, it became clear that Vaira, the future head of the company, was falling behind her future employees. Yet Vaira did not give up. She worked tirelessly to shape her reputation within the company, spending a fortune on a pair of Mag Lev mechanical arms, capable of manipulating Hard Light just as an architect would. Through this assistive design, she was able to compete against regular architects and come off as an effortless, naturally gifted professional. As Vaira Mascaraded through her education, she slowly moved up the corporate ladder outside Vishkar. She insisted on taking on the challenge outside the family business, working on private equity to perfect her own negotiation skills and reinforce her public image, so that once she returned, she would be seen as worthy of taking the family mantle.

events.name: The Atlantic Arcology
events.description:
The Martins had been some of Overwatch's most respected and valued members since the founding of the organization back during the Crisis. And within Overwatch, they have achieved massive accomplishments on a global scale after founding the Ecopoint program. However, the couple was not tied to the organization and in its later years they moved on from it to begin a project of their own.

After leaving Overwatch behind, the Martins formed The Collective. A scientific research nonprofit organization that could operate outside a politically regulated scope. It was meant to be a community made by scientists, for scientists, where every member contributed anything they could, and all of them shared the fruit of everyone's labor. This Utopic idea would later develop into the Atlantic Arcology, a massive sea spire made out of durovidro, that would become the home of the collective and anyone from the outer world who wished to be a part of their dream. Arcology became a self-sustaining, eco-friendly city that was not tethered to global politics, where scientists could continue their research without fears of funding or blockades, all with the objective of benefiting humanity as a whole. Many of the world's preeminent scholars and artists would end up sailing to the Spire, especially those seeking political asylum. As a result, it also went on to become a haven for Omnic refugees across the world, and the Collective welcomed them with open arms. Aside from the recurrent presence of curious whales migrating near the facilities and disrupting traffic in and out of the city, Arcology was a massive success for the scientific community, and showed many that Overwatch's goals could be pursued without their involvement.

events.name: Idina Troopers
events.description:
As the Scourge of Numbani continues its rampage across the city. Numbani would choose to remain independent, in spite of Overwatch's efforts to assist with the problem from the outside. To counter the warmonger's attacks, the city funded the creation of an early warning system, as well as the upgrading and reprogramming of a squadron of OR14 Idina line warbots. The reborn OR15 security robots would serve as the city's primary defense to external threats and oppose Doomfist's incursions into the city, to mixed results. 

Between Numbani's own defense force and Overwatch dismantling his lieutenants on the outside, Adeyemi found himself looking for more mercenaries to bolster his ranks. Among these new recruits came in Akande Ogundimu, the heir of Ogundimu Industries. Akande had lost his right arm during the crisis, and while his company provided him with cybernetics, the prosthetics deemed him to have an unfair advantage in martial arts competitions, forcing him to retire from fighting. Following the injury, Ogundimu grew into a bitter man who blamed the world for refusing him to participate in the sport he loved, and so he would come across Adeyemi, who offered him a place with his lieutenants. Akande was wary, but intrigued by learning more about the Doomfist's gauntlet and his connections to a shadow group of benefactors, going by the name of Talon.

events.name: Taking the Throne
events.description:
Every few seasons, Junketown hosts the Reckoning. A week-long festivity where junkers from all the outback could feast endlessly, and take part in brawls and shows of strength. The main event of the Reckoning consisted of a free for all in the Scrapyard. A battle for the death in which challengers use all sorts of gadgets and machinery to gain an open hand. Anyone may publicly challenge the reigning monarch to a battle for the crown, provided they were the last man standing in the brawl.

King Mason had gone undefeated for several years, reigning as a tyrant and stomping over allies. The heads of each Junker faction had long tried to dethrone him and take the crown for themselves, but they would all be eclipsed by a certain newcomer. Odessa Stone, an exiled wastelander, made her way into the city and challenged Mason. In her recounting debut, she faced Mari of the Thinkers and Geiger of the Scavengers, and was able to tear through both, while still showing them mercy. Odessa valued connections and loyalty, and could tell the faction leaders did not support their King. As such, she wished to show all Junkers the kind of leader she could be. The match was also being broadcast across the Outback, which she counted on to gain clout and respect with the masses. After defeating all the contenders, Mason himself came down from the top of the scrapyard to take on the wastelander.

After a fierce battle that followed pushed Mason into a corner, as his true colors showed at his worst when he activated the Scrapyard's Magnetism in a desperate move to end the fight. As the crowd turned on Mason for his recklessness, the leaders took Odessa's side, since she spared both. With the magnet down, Odessa was able to defeat the King, and win over support from the crowd, becoming the new Queen of the Junkers.

events.name: Project Echo Goes Online
events.description:
After Liao's work in Busan, she retreated back to Overwatch's research facility in Oslo. Years of work with Overwatch had slowly allowed her to gain support and leniency to work on her passion through passion, Artificial Intelligence. Following the success of the Athena AI, Liao would go on to work on several more complex projects in secrecy. Documentation on the rest of the projects following Athena is heavily classified, but we do know of her fifth and last, Project: Echo.

On February 5th of 2065, Project Echo went online for the first time. Her masterpiece, her legacy, her redemption, her apology all packaged into a perfect machine. Liao dreamed of one day improving on the design of Omnics, and possibly even replicating Aurora's sentience for years, but even after proving herself, Overwatch limited her research. In the end, Echo was designed as a multi-role adaptive robot that could learn by replicating behavior, using a chassis with Hard light projectors that would allow Echo to duplicate the form of those she scanned, not just learning from their behavior but imitating them to a T. In the end, what was supposed to push the boundaries of sentience once more, was decimated into yet another tool for Overwatch to deploy in missions.

events.name: When Dragons Fall
events.description:
After years of pushback from many governments, and seeing the sucess of their continous "vacations" Reyes approached Morrison with the idea of expanding his division to deal with more questionable operations, taking care of things outside of the protocol and away from the public eye. With Morrion's approval, Blackwatch as we know it, was born. The small cover up division Reyes had been running expanded into a midsize initiative, expanding with several bases and gaining recruitment under Amari's selection. During these hirings, many people who would have otherwise not passed Overwatch's regular screenings would go on to become part of Reyes' staff, often kept secret from Ana and Jack. Perhaps the most well known of these fringe hires was Genji Shimada.

Overwatch was keeping an eye on the Shimada clan for years, but their influence in the Kanezaka region made them hard to take down legally. That was until the assassination of the clan leader, Sojiro Shimada, at the hands of a rival gang, the Hashimoto. Ever since his death, the clan began to expand its criminal activities, and their carelessness marked them as a target for Overwatch to take down. During initial scouting and intel gathering, Ziegler and Sarioglu were notified of a man who had been critically injured. The man turned out to be Genji Shimada, heir to the clan, and was taken into Overwatch's care as a potential valuable source of information. 
Following his father's death, the elders of the clan took control and began to put pressure on his older brother, Hanzo, to take control as the new leader. For the longest time, Genji took little interest in the illegal activities of the clan and spent his days pursuing a playboy lifestyle. The elders considered him a liability and resented Sojiro's softness, so when Hanzo took control of the clan, they instructed him to straighten up his own brother. Genji refused, and tensions grew between the two brothers, building to a violent confrontation with a blade which left Genji on the verge of death. After hearing his story, Gabriel Reyes pitched him the idea of joining forces. If Genji agreed to work with Blackwatch to take down the rest of the Clan, Overwatch could rebuild his body with cybernetics.

Angela Ziegler, the head of the medical division and the one in charge of Genji's care, objected to the coercion of his patient, but Genji, enraged by what his brother had done and without much of a choice in his mutilated state, accepted the offer. Ziegler was opposed to the procedure, worrying about the weaponizing of the augmentations she would need to develop for Genji, but ultimately committed when given the direct order to do so. With the full scope of the medical team, Ziegler replaced the man's falling organs with cybernetics, mostly based on the ones Sojourn herself relied on. Once he was stable, they began to augment his body and turned him into a living weapon. Genji and Angela formed a very close bond over the several months that passed during the procedure, and this connection was pivotal in Genji's future reassignment to the strike team.

events.name: Out There
events.description:
Among the Apes in the Lunar Colony, one of the gorillas was displaying rapid brain development faster than the rest. Specimen 28, was taken under the wing of Dr. Harold Winston, who took care of the Gorilla almost like a son, nourishing his curiosity with science and tales of human ingenuity back on earth. The young gorilla spent his days assisting the Doctor on experiments, away from the other apes, as he had become an outcast among the other apes. At one point, 28 would be visited by a peculiar, small intruder. Specimen 8, Hammond, had escaped through the vents, and he had just interrupted his plans.

Not long before they first met, Hammond realized that Winston was just like him, another survivor of the genetic therapy who was showing intelligence faster than his peers, and he was struck with a sense of sympathy, at seeing someone just as alone as he was. He was moved by the ape's innocence and sense of wonder, and the two quickly became friends. During his nightly escapades, Hammond would sneak treats like candy and peanut butter, and aid the gorilla in fixing things that the others break. In return, the ape showed him how to dream of a better life. The pair spent hours watching the giant blue marble just outside the window of the habitat, wondering what awaited them there. 

The pair wished to one day leave the base together and explore this distant world, and it just so happened that Hammond's escapades were not just to visit his friend. All this time, he had been tinkering with nuts and bolts, learning how the machinery in the base work, and he quickly fancied himself a mechanic. Even if the scientists found him every time he escaped his cell, none of them had a clue about the real purpose of his exploration of the base. He was set on leaving the moon, and taking Specimen 28 with him.

events.name: Cursed
events.description:
Mizuki's home life continues to deteriorate, as misfortune continued to haunt the family. Dead after death had taken a toll on Mizuki's father as he felt further into superstitions and scams. Failing to cope with the tragedies that marked his life, he continued to blame it on malevolent spirits, and taught his child to fear them. In an attempt to escape their curse, the two would move out of Fukuoka and into Tokyo, but as his father's mental state continued to worsen, he would begin to accumulate debt from the many scams, lucky charms and exorcists that he had paid for as a desperate attempt to ward off the ghouls.

Mizuki's father would begin to take loans from the Hashimoto Clan, the local criminal group who called Tokyo their home turf. As the family plunged further into debt, Mizuki could only watch in fear, until the day his father was found dead, leaving him to fend for himself. At only nine years old, Mizuki was left as an orphan, and inherited mountains' worth of debt to the Hashimoto. The young kid had internalized the years' worth of trauma that his father inflicted upon the family, he believed the curse would claim his own life next. As the Hashimoto barged in to collect the debts, the Elders would take a keen interest in the young boy. The clan showed Mizuki mercy by forgiving his debt if he joined their organization, and without nowhere else to go, Mizuki accepted this fate reluctantly.

events.name: Search and Rescue
events.description:
With Overwatch expanding all of its departments from Blackwatch to the Ecopoint program, Ana Amari aided each division in the recruitment process and one. Generally, most of the supportive staff tended to be overshadowed by the Strike team in the public eye, but one key addition gained notoriety even among the Strike team itself: Freja Skov, the daughter of a Dannish diplomat who played a key role in her country's defense during the Omnic Crisis. She had admired her mother's work for the longest time, but instead of following in her footsteps, she wished to save people on the ground alongside other operatives. After graduating from University with a criminology degree, Freja joined her local search and rescue team to gain experience in the field. It was here where she developed outstanding skills at tracking down anyone, in any place, as she felt in love with the rush of seeing a job through the end, and making a difference hands on.

For the longest time, Freja dedicated herself to chasing that gratifying feeling, and her determination began to open many doors. Ana Amari approached Freja to join a burgeoning search and rescue division among the Overwatch support personnel. While in Overwatch, Freja would quickly gain notoriety for her unmatched excellence on the job, with many considering her the best tracker in the world. Freja would go on to become personally requested as an assiting operative by several members of the Strike team, like Reinhardt, Mercy and particularly Emre Sarioglu, who she grew especially close with, often taking trips to Istanbul together while on break from duty.

events.name: Takeover
events.description:
For years, Akande Ogundimu had trained under the wing of Akinjide Adeyemi, as one of the best lieutenants. His involvement was known by Numbani's security forces, but he was smart enough to keep distance away from more active conflicts to avoid being apprehended. As Ogundimu became one of Adeyemi's most trusted confidants, behind his back, Akande was thinking far bigger than he could ever have imagined. Akande was a well-studied visionary, who had grown to adopt a certain philosophy as his personal mantra: Humanity evolves through conflict.  =It was conflict like the pandemics earlier in the century that pushed medical advances to their limits. It was conflict like the ecological impact of humanity that led to the hundreds of inventions that made life better. It was a conflict like the Omnic Crisis that allowed humanity to come together with Overwatch, and push through an enemy larger than any nation on their own. It was conflict that prevented him from sports, and allowed him to realize his true calling. Akande was meant to spread conflict across the world, for the betterment of humanity.

Akande had grown connections within Talon's council through Adeyemi's network of mercenaries, and within the council they saw him as a potentially valuable asset. Before long, Ogundimu made his choice. He turned against his master and after a brutal face off, he was able to kill him, taking the Gauntlet and the mantle of Doomfist for himself. Akande was already respected within Talon, but between his vision, and now the imposing force of the Gauntlet, the members of the council would have little option but to fall in line at his command. After all, it would be easier to take advantage of governments and industry amid the chaos of ongoing conflicts, so while the new Doomfist pursued an ideology, his closest allies stuck around purely to profit from his crusade.

Under Doomfist, Talon would shift from being an amorphous, silent force that hijacked power across regions, to a loud, imposing terrorist that quickly proved to be Overwatch's primary obstacle in their peacekeeping duties. The organization was actively growing at this time as well. Research teams began to raise warnings of fringe scientists being recruited by Talon agents, and in some cases, reputable researchers getting kidnapped. Perhaps a more direct blow would be the beggining of a recruitment campaign across the Carribean Sea, as many experienced personnel from the Caribbean Coalition ranks began to be lured by the calling of Talon. Reyes and Lacroix became aware of the change in leadership and even obtained partial footage of the battle. The subject became a main priority for their research, but soon enough, Overwatch would have their hands full from dealing with Talon.

events.name: Lost in Time
events.description:
Overwatch's research team was relatively quieter than the many controversies the Strike team and Blackwatch would eventually be remembered for, but one of their many experiments would become one of the things the organization became the most known for. Lena Oxton was at the time a young pilot in the Brittish Royal Air Force and had just enlisted into Overwatch for trials of their next generation, teleporting fighter jet, the Splitstream. Lena might have been pretty young at the time, but her uniquely fast reflexes, hand coordination and love for flying at high speeds made her the most suited choice for the test that Britain could offer. 

Unfortunately, during the first test flight, the ship's teleportation matrix would malfunction, and suddenly both Oxton and the jet vanished. Lena was presumed dead, and the incident did not bring any good publicity for Overwatch, but what the scientists did not expect at the time was that Lena's disappearance was a far more complex, rare event than merely vanishing in the accident.

Oxton's molecular structure had become desynchronized from the regular flow of time and space, a condition that would later be called "Chronal Disassociation." In short, Lena was a living ghost, disappearing for hours and days at a time, and whenever she was visible in our present, it was often at completely different places, and she could barely maintain a physical form for long. Lena would remain in this limbo state for about two whole years, in which her body showed no signs of aging while disassociated from our timeline. After her first reappearance in the months after the accident, Shira Wexler, the lead designer of the jet, took full responsibility for her condition and dedicated her team's efforts to finding a cure.

events.name: Shimada Takedown
events.description:
After months of treatment, Genji's body was fully reconstructed, and the man had turned into a living weapon, filled with rage and eager to take down his family's business. Taking down the Shimada, a group so deeply rooted in the local community's culture and politics, required a stealth operation that only Blackwatch could see through the end, and with Genji's intel, they would be able to get the upper hand. After months of preparation, Reyes, Lacroix, Genji and Cassidy strategized strikes to arrest several of the major players left in the clan, disrupting their operations piece by piece until the entire Empire began crumbling from within. 

To make matters easier, the Clan had also lost their other heir, not long after Genji. Hanzo Shimada had abandoned his duties, feeling ashamed by his actions, since, as far as he knew, he had killed his brother in cold blood at the orders of a bunch of old men who were supposed to serve him. They say the man became a vagabond, taking his father's Anima storm bow with him as a memento, he swore never to wield a blade again, and once he left Hanamura Castle and began to travel the world, seeking for ways to restore his honor and put his old life behind.

 Without solid leadership, the remaining members of the clan began turning on each other and resorting to more openly illegal activities like blackmailing schemes, which slowly fractured their reputation with the Kanezaka community, fueling their decline further. The long-lasting Shimada Clan had been completely dismantled from the shadows, and although Genji craved more, and actively pursued the take-down of other rival gangs in the region like the Hashimoto, the conditions that allowed for the fall of the Shimada were ultimately too circumstantial. Overwatch would not be as lucky as they were here if they tried to take on some of the bigger clans of the broader Tokyo region.

events.name: Blacklisted
events.description:
After Genji's recruitment into Blackwatch, Angela began to file many complaints against Reyes, stating that his actions went against everything that Overwatch stood for. Vivian was also openly opposed to Blackwatch, though given her special ops background, she showed more appreciation towards Reyes and what he could accomplish, just less about the extralegal nature of his actions. While Morrison noted these complaints and asked Reyes to avoid antagonizing them further, Reyes immediately prioritized finding a new medical official to specialize in Blackwatch operations. He came across a research paper released by an ambitious geneticist, Moira O'Deorain, DNA alteration at a cellular level. The methodology shared a lot of principles with the Soldier Enhancement Program, but innovated on them by making some morally questionable choices, where the treatment would cause cells to continually tear themselves apart and immediately regenerate, degenerating and repeating the subject's entire body through sheer biological will.

Reyes flagged Moira as worth watching, but not long after, a condemnation by the medical staff was released, before he could run her credentials. Moira's work was problematic in the scientific community, with plenty of researchers unable to replicate the results, and the ethical shortfalls of her work resulted in growing dissent among her peers. This was not the promising step forward in human potential she had promised; it was mere shoddy science. Moira was dismissed from the University of Dublin, seen as a liability, and when she attempted to work in the private sector, her name had been blacklisted and no reputable group would employ her. Through all this, Ziegler specifically spoke out against O'Deorain, and Reyes ignored each and every remark. 

Gabriel sent an offer to Moira, promising resources, staff and a laboratory of her own, and while initially suspicious, the idea of rubbing her success in the faces of those who destroyed her career lured her in. Moira worked in the shadows, developing new weapons and technology for Blackwatch, even testing her genetic research on Gabriel himself. Since he was a SEP subject, it allowed her further her progress emulating the original formula. Her employment was kept under absolute secrecy, and unlike most other Blackwatch agents, she was not openly associated with Overwatch's primary organization. If Blackwatch was Overwatch's dirty little secret, Moira was theirs.

events.name: Cold Snap
events.description:
Shortly after O'Deorain's recruitment, Overwatch would face a devastating loss in Ecopoint Antartica. The scientific team on the base dedicated their studies to finding and replicating anomalous weather conditions, but not even with their equipment and knowledge were they able to foresee the quick formation of a massive superstorm in the region. An abnormally huge blizzard trapped the team within the base in unlivable conditions, and made evacuation efforts extremely risky. The Ecopoint facilities were equipped with cryo sleep technology, allowing the team to freeze their bodies and enter a stasis, which should have allowed them to survive until the storm passed. These kinds of storms had happened before, and it had become standard procedure, but this particular blizzard was worse than any projections could have estimated.

As the storm raged on, the team remained in cryo stasis, getting past the survival time window, where the system should have forced them to wake up, in the middle of the never-ending blizzard. Months passed, and the storm did not dissipate. Any attempts to establish communication failed, so the team was pressured to death. Overwatch would attempt to retrieve their bodies on multiple occasions, but the storm's anomalous persistence prevented access to the base for a full year. During one of the many rescue attempts, an Icebreaker ship, the Resolute, would crash into the Ice, and the rescue team barely managed to escape with their lives. The anomaly continued and Overwatch was forced to stop further retrieval attempts, lest they lose more agents.

All of Overwatch entered a mourning period, with the Ecopoint bases rushing to improve security protocols in case of evacuation, and the UN calling for a full accounting of the records for review. Hector and Claudio joined their old partners in mourning the loss of the entire Antarctica team, and those who died trying to get their bodies back.

events.name: Biolight
events.description:
As Niran grew older, he would use his parent's wealth to travel the world during his breaks from the architecture academy. While exploring the wilderness of the globe, he was able to experience the turmoil that existed in the world outside his bubble of wealth. Animals and forests dying, in part due to Vishkar's own expansion, war, illness and poverty everywhere he looked, with people who needed help being actively ignored, entire communities riddled with pain. Niran became determined to use his talents to fix the world, and began to experiment with healing applications of hardlight. After months of research at the Architech Academy, Niran used his knowledge and fascination of botany. Niran would push the limits of hardlight design into something new entirely. A self-replicating biomass made from hardlight. A living, organic material manifested from light structures, capable of repairing tissue near its light in the blink of an eye. Biolight was a revolutionary discovery that could change the world and bring balance back between man and nature. However, he would soon discover that was not possible under Vishkar. 

During his research at the Academy, he would come to discover the notes of Vishwakarma Bhatt, the late founder of the corporation, as well as his dream of providing hardlight freely to the world. Niran wished Biolight would remain accessible to all as well, but seeing how quick the company was to ignore their founder's wishes, choosing to place restrictions on the technology and profit from their monopoly, Niran knew the same fate would await his discovery.  When he unveiled the technology, Vishkar began to claim full ownership of the invention, and Niran resisted. Against the demands of Vishkar's higher ups and the advice of his closest friend Satya, Niran fled the academy and took his research on Biolight with him. Overnight, he became a wanted man on a crusade against a powerful empire, and to make matters worse, his family would cut him off from their wealth to pressure him into turning in his discovery. Niran was alone and hunted, but not out of options. He pleaded for sanctuary in the Atlantic Arcology, where he would be received by a supportive community of like-minded individuals. The nature of the Collective placed him outside global laws and prevented Vishkar from pursuing him further, and the assets of the Arcology allowed Niran to continue his research independently, working alongside the other members of the collective to bring his power to the world, free of charge.

events.name: Uneasy Alliance
events.description:
Soon after his takeover of Talon, Doomfist began his campaign of conflict by arranging a series of attacks on powerful figures across the globe. Maximillien, the head of the ever-growing Monte Carlo, was naturally a tantalizing target. The first assassin sent his way caused concern, but swiftly dealt with by his security staff. When the second assassin came, this was more flattering. Following this second attempt, the assassin would not return to Doomfist empty-handed, as they claimed the target proposed a deal. 

Not long after, Max found himself in the wreckage of his office, as the imposing figure of Doomfist himself towered over him. Max understood well the level of power he was dealing with, Doomfist was unquestioned and his crusade would not be stopped. All he could do if he had any hope of surviving, was join his side. Maximillien's deal allowed Akande access to his resources, money and connections, as well as his ability to manage Talon's assets effectively. In return, he asked to be allowed to live, as one of his council members. Talon's aid would also allow Maximilien to pursue more contentious negotiations, offering leverage through brute force.

Lucky for Maximillien, Doomfist respected his perseverance, his confidence and his ability to adapt, the deal was made, and Max dragged all of Monte Carlo into Talon's umbrella, just to save his own life. The deal, however, was still a losing hand for Max. At La Coterie, control was everything, and Max had long enjoyed being the one pulling the strings of everyone who was once above him. The house always wins, but if Talon took the house, what would be left of him?

events.name: Lunar Revolt
events.description:
The Horizon Lunar Colony had stood strong for decades, it saw a changing world, survived the Crisis, and now was home to a generation of increasingly smarter Apes. The experiments pursued by the Horizon team, however, were extremely morally dubious in nature, and the animals themselves grew to resent their handlers for their actions. By this time, Dr. Chao, the head of the research, had returned to Earth, as long-term activity on the moon turned her bones feeble, unable to walk on land, or to get cybernetics, she had moved back to Lijiang Tower's administrative sectors, but the experiments continued without her, and the researchers soon would realize the price for crossing such ethical barriers.

Communication with Horizon was lost abruptly and never restored. What little is known is that the Apes ead an uprising against the staff, killing every single scientist, and claiming control of the colony for themselves. Specimen 28, a gorilla who grew attached to his caretaker, Harold Winston, saw his world crumble apart as his peers killed his teacher. 28 took his mentor's name into his memory and, in the middle of the chaos, he was able to find a nearly finished, makeshift rocket in the base. After putting the finishing touches, he was able to launch the pod and escape to Earth. Little did he know that vessel was actually Hammond's, and he had worked on it for months hoping that they could escape together. Ingenious as always, Hammond was able to attach a spare capsule to Winston's vessel, and secretly hitched a ride when they blasted off. However, the cable broke when they entered the Atmosphere, and Hammond landed in a completely different spot than Winston, who had no idea his friend was following close.

Winston's ship would be intercepted by Overwatch agents in Gibraltar. After making landfall, the team was surprised to find he could address them in perfect English, as he required urgent medical assistance and lost consciousness soon after. After recovery, Overwatch made controversial headlines with the recruitment of their first fully non-human member. Winston proved himself highly intelligent, strong and agile, and when he requested to join the organization, he himself presented a case for personhood in court, following the precedents set by the Omnic Rights movement. Winston's reception was mixed at the start due to the ethically questionable nature of his mere existence, but he quickly proved to be an invaluable ally for the organization, as well as one of the most charismatic faces of the Strike team, becoming the face of the "Overwatch Adventures" cartoon, and by far the most marketable agent with a line of pluses and toys of his own.

events.name: Intrepit Adventurers
events.description:
The Crisis left behind a broken world, and with it priceless history and icons of cultural value were lost. While many cities rebuilt themselves and archen back to the classical periods, it became all the more evident to many across the world how important it was to preserve history, what was left of it anyway. This is how the Wayfinder Society came to be, a group of archeologists and historians from around the globe, coming together to share resources and connections, all with the goal of preparing expeditions all over the world, to retrieve artifacts and find clues from the past to use in the present.

Sloan Cameron had spent their life traveling with their family, getting to see all corners of the world and exploring their culture and history each time they arrived at a new place. This innate curiosity as well as a keen eye for finding unearthed trinkets drew them to the Wayfinders. At age sixteen, they began an apprenticeship with the group, and never looked back. Sloan would adopt the nickname "Venture" while operating in digsites, and as they joined expeditions through ancient ruins and dormant Omniums, their unfettered bravery and persistence in dangerous situations would make them stand out among many other Wayfinders. Sloan would often return from dig sites covered in dirt, bruises and local fauna, but always with a smile on their face, and it was this passion and dedication that allowed them to take increasingly dangerous and exciting expeditions with the Wayfinders.

events.name: Hostage
events.description:
Overwatch had taken down the Shimada clan, but in doing so it merely caused a power vacuum in the Kanezaka region. The illegal activities were not the only priority of the clan, as they also saw themselves as the protectors of Kanezaka for generations. In the words of their late leader, if the relationship the clan has with the people of the region is anything less than mutually beneficial, they were nothing more than a parasite. This social contract had stood strong for generations, as the Shimada kept larger, more dangerous gangs at bay, but as their empire crumbled and their operations became more openly shady, the people turned their backs on them, only to soon realize they were the lesser of two evils.

The Hashimoto clan had long been the main rival crime family to the Shimada, and after Overwatch left, they began a takeover of Kanezaka, placing Keigo Kuroura in charge of overseeing the region. As a stronger, more stable group, Overwatch did not have the same level of leverage against them that they did with the Shimada, so they were essentially able to reign uncontested. In spite of this, however, the Hashimoto quickly realized there was one potential threat lingering after the fall of the Shimada, the Yamagami Blades, particularly Toshiro himself, who was responsible for the powerful weaponry that allowed the Shimada to hold their ground for so long.  The clan act quickly, kidnapping Toshiro and forcing him to supply their syndicate with Anima weapons of their own. Alone and with her hands tied, Asa saw no other choice but to send Kiriko away, changing her last name to Kamori, Asa's birth name, and taking her to live with her grandmother to protect her from the clan.

While he was a hostage, Toshiro had not yet surrendered himself fully to the Hashimoto. The man would agree to their demands for Anima weapons to keep himself alive, but he purposely butchered his own work, producing dismal blades with unstable Anima fields, effectively declawing the wielders from the potential danger they could pose. In his time at the Neon Junction of Akihabara, he would often be joined by Mizuki, who was still far too young to take part in the clan's criminal dealings, and was often left behind in the stronghold. Mizuki kept Toshiro company, and in return, Toshiro's kindness was able to balance his harsh walk of life as he got older and began to take part in clan duties.

secondaryCountryPlaces.reasoning: The Hashimoto expand into Kanezaka after the Shimada takedown

events.name: Chronal Dissociation
events.description:
Overwatch generally saw Winston as a possible fresh start, an outsider who had no preconceptions about Omnics, and with an idealized vision of human culture and history. He never saw the world for what it was, he dared to see what it could be. Since he lacked the assumptions of what was or not possible, he was the perfect mind to tackle the Oxton case. Alongside Wexler's team, Winston studied Lena's Chronal disassociation condition and began work on a device that he called the Chronal anchor. This harness was intended to pull Oxton back into the normal flow of time, and keep her from blipping out again. However, when tested, the device was only capable of keeping Lena anchored in space, not time. Her body was no longer reappearing in entirely different places, but it still vanished from the time flow uncontrollably. Still, progress was still progress.

During this testing, Winston realized that the matrix of the device was building up an unknown form of energy the longer Lena was kept anchored. Eventually, this buildup reaches a critical amount, causing Oxton's time fluctuations. After learning this, he scrapped the original design in favor of a new device called the Chronal Accelerator. This new iteration harnessed the mounting energy into a limited, controlled disassociation, essentially allowing Oxton to experience localized time travel at will. The device kept Lena anchored in time, and as energy built, she could use it to willingly blink or rewind back her own relationship with time and space, releasing the buildup without losing control. Oxton was stable and capable of living a somewhat normal life as long as she was in proximity to a Chronal Accelerator, but she realized that this gift of hers could be used to help Overwatch and the world. So, after being cleared from test runs, Lena began training to become part of the Strike team alongside Winston, who she grew to be close friends with. After all the time, he worked on a solution to her problem. Due to the trails of light left by the Chronal accelerator as she blinked through time, she would earn the call sign Tracer.

events.name: Shrine Maiden
events.description:
Under the care of her grandmother, Kiriko would forge a close and lasting connection with the Fox Spirit, not just because of her Miko duties, but also through the Fox's anima that her father had created. Her grandma would often let Kiriko borrow the Anima ofuda as she taught her how to prepare her tools for Miko duties, and Kiriko quickly developed a strong sense of justice and empathy, using the anima's power to take on bullies and thugs, protecting those in need and healing them with her grandma's teachings.

Kiriko would spend most of her adolescent years as her grandma's assistant, while her parents, separated by the clan, worked together to find a way to take them down. For years, Toshiro was able to maintain communication with Asa exclusively through letters, in which he fed his wife intel about Hashimoto's plans. For years, Kanezaka would suffer through injustice and exploitation at the hands of the new reigning clan, and Kiriko was not blind to the city's pain. Not just as the shrine maiden and a follower of Kanezaka's local deity, but also as a close pupil of the Shimada's teachings. While the old clan was unmistakably corrupt and their activies were as bad as the Hashimotos, Sojiro's stories and values left a deep mark on Kiriko, arguably more than they ever did on his own children. She would grow up to be a strong, caring leader, and a fitting protector for the city, as she vowed to one day take on the Hashimoto, rescue her father, and restore peace to the region.

events.name: Escalation
events.description:
Null Sector activity continued across the world, inspiring many Omnics to follow Ramattra's teachings, especially following the groundbreaking of Turing Green, which many followers of Mondatta saw as a new start for Omnics. Null Sector, in contrast, took the idea as an insult, throwing a bone at his people to try and act like it absolved them from years worth of injustice. As momentum grew, the group radicalized from a small cell of liberators, to a fully militarized operation. The escalation meant many of his initial supporters soon began to leave the group behind, but his resolve to bring justice for all Omnics only grew stronger with each ally he lost.

He decided to bring his closest allies, Nameless, Zera and Lanet, on a journey across the depths of a freezing wasteland, where they found the very same Anubis made Omnium, where Ramattra was born. His plan was to use the facility to create an army, and use it to speak to humans in a language they can understand, violence. For the next two years, the group would produce entire battalions of Crisis-era units at Lanet's supervision, and Ramattra himself would upgrade his own design combining Shambali technology with an invention of his own, Nanites, microscopic machines capable of cooperation and assembly of different structures, including extra reinforced limbs for close combat. As his army grew, Ramattra already had his eyes on the first targer of his revolution, but Lanet urged caution, noting the antiquated designs needed more work if they wanted to get things right.

During this back-and-forward, tension built up over philosophical differences amongst the group, as Ramattra continuously refused to involve other Omnics and rally forces outside his mindless troops. While Nameless and Zera were used to appealing to the Omnic masses, and wished to allow their people to fight alongside them, Ramattra was tired of bloodshed. He saw himself as a liberator, not a general. It was because of this that he chose to manufacture an army of robots, instead of using his influence to command his supporters, as he wished to keep sentient Omnics away from active conflict as much as possible. This led to constant argument with his allies, who saw their leader radicalize further into a lonely crusade.

events.name: Champion
events.description:
While Winston was intercepted by Overwatch and found a new home with the organization, Hammond's escape pod would take him to a far more hostile land. Crashing in the middle of the Australian Outback, Hammond used his ingenuity to survive the cruel wasteland. Scrapping debris and metal Omnics, he was able to modify the escape pod into a mobile unit for him to pilot around.

He would eventually make his way into Junkertown and learn about their culture, growing fascinated by the Scrapyard Arena and seeing it as an opportunity to prove himself. Using his knowledge of physics, and available weaponry, his mech became a rolling ball of death, which he used to climb up the ranks of the city. Going by the name of Wrecking Ball, Hammond became a celebrity among Junkers, though his identity remained secret as no one ever saw him outside the mech. Soon enough, Wrecking Ball went from contender, to uncontested champion, becoming a close ally and confidant to the Junker Queen herself, the first person to learn of his true identity. 

Hammond had found a happy, chaotic home among the Junkers and while he was still hurt by Winston abandoning him, he never really looked back now that he had his new life. On occasion, he would wonder out of the city to explore the wider world and acquire parts of his inventions, but of course, as the champion of the Scrapyard, he would always come back to defend his title.

secondaryCountryPlaces.reasoning: Wrecking ball becomes the Champion of the Scrapyard
